content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

September 29, 2006

Colleen C. McAndrews
Bell, McAndrews & Hiltachk, LLP
455 Capitol Mall, Suite 801
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-06-180

Dear Ms. McAndrews:

This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of "Californians
Against the Statewide Parcel Property Tax, No on 88, A Project of the Howard Jarvis
Taxpayers Association and the Small Business Action Committee" regarding the
campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

Under the advertisement disclosure provisions of the Act, may a disclosure
statement be shortened when one of the major contributors is a sponsored committee with
the same sponsor as the sponsored committee that is running the advertisement, so as to
avoid repeating the name of the sponsor?

CONCLUSION

No. The Act does not provide an exception in its advertisement disclosure
provisions that would allow for elimination of part of a committee's or person's name
when that committee or person is required to be identified.

FACTS

"Californians Against the Statewide Parcel Property Tax, No on 88, A Project of
the Howard Jarvis Taxpayers Association and the Small Business Action Committee" is
the current name of a sponsored committee ("Committee") of which you are the treasurer

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. A-06-180
Page No. 2

and legal counsel. The Committee was formed as a primarily formed sponsored ballot
measure committee to oppose Proposition 88 on the November 2006 ballot.

The Committee sponsors are two associations organized under Internal Revenue
Code section 501(c)(4), each of which conduct political activity in California under other
sponsored committees, which are registered pursuant to the provisions of the Act.

The Committee intends to start production of radio spots (30 second and 15
second) and has asked you to advise them on the proper disclaimer. The spots will not be
broadcast until several weeks from now.

The top two donors of contributions of $50,000 or more, as of the date of
September 19, 2006, are a long standing sponsored general purpose recipient committee,
"No New Taxes, A Project of the Howard Jarvis Taxpayers Association," and a major
donor, "The American Resort Development Association – Resort Owners Coalition."

There is a possibility that the PAC of the other sponsor of the Committee, the
Small Business Action Committee, may become one of the top two $50,000 donors by
the time the radio spots are broadcast.²

No New Taxes is controlled by the same officers as the Howard Jarvis Taxpayers
Association, and Small Business Action Committee PAC is controlled by the same
officers as the Small Business Action Committee.

Your client has asked how the name for the disclaimer may be shortened so as not
to absorb the entire time allotted for the radio advertisement.

ANALYSIS

Section 84504(c) provides that "[a]ny committee which supports or opposes a
ballot measure, shall print or broadcast its name as provided in this section as part of any
advertisement or other paid public statement."³ Section 84504(a) provides:

"(a) Any committee that supports or opposes one or more ballot
measures shall name and identify itself using a name or phrase that clearly
identifies the economic or other special interest of its major donors of fifty
thousand dollars or more in any references to the committee required by
law ..."

_______________
² In a subsequent telephone conversation, you also indicated that there is also the possibility that
the sponsors made change before the radio advertisement is aired, which would require you to File an
amended Statement of Organization, thereby changing the name of the committee. If this is the case, you
may seek further advice, if necessary, as to how that change may affect our analysis.
³ Advertisement means "any general or public advertisement which is authorized and paid for by a
person or committee for the purpose of supporting or opposing a candidate for elective office or a ballot
measure or ballot measures" and includes "[p]rogramming received by a television or radio." (Section
84501(a) and regulation 18450.1(a)(1).)

File No. A-06-180
Page No. 3

Regulation 18402(c) provides:

(c) Whenever identification of a committee is required by law, the
identification shall include the full name of the committee as required in
the statement of organization.

(1) In the case of a sponsored committee, the statement of
organization shall include the name of the committee as provided in 2 Cal.
Code Regs. section 18419.

(2) For purposes of Government Code section 84504, in the case of
a committee primarily formed to support or oppose a ballot measure, the
committee name shall clearly identify the economic or other special
interest of the committee's major donors of $50,000 or more.

(A) If candidates or their controlled committees, as a group or
individually are major contributors of $50,000 or more, the primarily
formed committee name shall include the controlling candidate's name.

(B) If the major donors of $50,000 or more share a common
employer, the identity of the employer shall also be disclosed in the name
of the primarily formed committee.

Therefore, as outlined above, the full name of the committee, "Californians
Against the Statewide Parcel Property Tax, No on 88, A Project of the Howard Jarvis
Taxpayers Association and the Small Business Action Committee"("No on 88
Committee") must be identified as part of the broadcast advertisement.⁴

Additionally, section 84503(a) requires that an advertisement for or against any
ballot measure include a disclosure statement identifying any person⁵ whose cumulative
contributions are fifty thousand dollars or more. If there are more than two donors at that
level, only the top two are required to be disclosed. (Section 84503(b).)

You have indicated that, currently, the top two donors over $50,000 are a
recipient committee – "No New Taxes, A Project of the Howard Jarvis Taxpayers
Association" and a major donor – "The American Resort Development Association –
Resort Owners Coalition," but that another committee, "The Small Business Action
Committee PAC," may replace the American Resort Development Association – Resort

⁴ In a subsequent telephone conversation with this office you mentioned that the committee may
no longer be a sponsored committee. If this is the case, you may amend your Statement of Organization to
reflect the change in name, and the new name would then need to be provided as part of the broadcast.
⁵ "Person" means any individual, proprietorship, firm partnership, joint venture, syndicate,
business trust, company, corporation, limited liability company, association, committee, and any other
organization or group of person acting in concert." (Section 82047).

File No. A-06-180
Page No. 4

Owners Coalition as the second largest contributor by the time the advertisements are
run. Each of these entities are persons as defined under the Act.

Regulation 18450.4(c) provides that the disclosure required under section 84503
shall include the name of the contributor or contributors of $50,000 or more.
Additionally:

"The disclosure shall explicitly indicate that the contributor or contributors
were major donors to the committee by stating, for example, "major
funding by" or "paid for by." In the case of a contributor that is a
committee pursuant to Government Code section 82013, subdivision (a),
the word "committee" shall be included in the disclosure." (Emphasis
added.)

Accordingly, under the provisions outlined above, the disclosure required under
the facts stated in your request would be as follows:

"Californians Against the Statewide Parcel Property Tax, No on 88, A
Project of the Howard Jarvis Taxpayers Association and the Small
Business Action Committee with major funding by the No New Taxes
Committee, A Project of the Howard Jarvis Taxpayers Association and by
[either][the American Resort Development Association – Resort Owners
Coalition [or] the Small Business Action Committee PAC [depending on
which entity is the second largest contributor at the time of the
advertisement.]"

For advertisements of 15 seconds or less, the disclosure need only include the
highest major contributor, in addition to the name of the ballot measure committee, in
identifying the "persons" that provided the major funding for the advertisement as required
by section 84503 and regulation 18450.4(c). (Section 84508.)

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: William J. Lenkeit
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

avoid repeating the name of the sponsor?

CONCLUSION: No. The Act does not provide an exception in its advertisement disclosure
provisions that would allow for elimination of part of a cornmittee,s or person,s name
when that committee or person is required to be identified.
